channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump taps Brooke Rollins to head USDA
item.description: Her selection comes just a day after Trump announced Scott Turner, another America First Policy Institute leader would join his administration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: White House mostly mum after Trump victory
item.description: The White House has taken its show largely behind closed doors after Vice President Harris’s stinging defeat to Donald Trump. Harris has all but disappeared from the public eye, jetting off to Hawaii after a few scarce appearances around Washington following her massive loss on Nov. 5.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's Republican Party is increasingly winning union voters. It's a shift seen in his labor pick
item.description: Working-class voters helped Republicans make steady election gains this year and expanded a coalition that increasingly includes rank-and-file union members.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump taps Texan Brooke Rollins as agriculture secretary
item.description: President-elect Trump tapped Brooke Rollins, who served in his first administration as director of the Office of American Innovation and acting director of the Domestic Policy Council, as his agriculture secretary.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dr. Oz's disputed health claims could jeopardize Senate confirmation
item.description: Oz's ties to online supplement brands and reported health misinformation could jeopardize his confirmation.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about Scott Turner, Trump's pick for housing secretary
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has selected Scott Turner to lead the U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development.
item.dc:creator: Adriana Gomez Licon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Blinken heads to last G7 meeting of Biden presidency with Ukraine and Mideast topping the agenda
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken is leaving on a diplomatic trip to Europe that includes a meeting of foreign ministers from the leading industrialized nations that will focus on the wars in Ukraine and the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bezos, Musk rivalry heats up ahead of Trump’s second term
item.description: A social media spat between Elon Musk and Jeff Bezos this week underscored a sharpening rivalry between two of the world’s richest men at a time when both are trying to grow their sprawling business empires in President-elect Trump’s second term.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump names Seb Gorka, Alex Wong to be senior national security staff
item.description: President-elect Trump has tapped his first term alums Alex Wong and Sebastian Gorka to join his national security team for his second go-around.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, NewsNation

item.title: Trump taps a Fox News personality, a surgeon and a former Congressman to lead public health agencies
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Friday nominated Dr. Marty Makary to lead the Food and Drug Administration, selecting a surgeon and author who gained national attention for opposing vaccine mandates and some other public health measures during the COVID-19 pandemic.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW PERRONE and AMANDA SEITZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: From CDC to labor secretary: See Trump's top picks for Cabinet roles
item.description: Trump announced a slew of cabinet announcement on Friday as he begins to shape his Cabinet for his second term.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News contributor Janette Nesheiwat tapped as Trump's surgeon general
item.description: President-elect Trump named Janette Nesheiwat, a Fox News contributor and medical doctor in New York and New Jersey, as his nominee for surgeon general in his second administration. "I am proud to announce that Dr. Janette Nesheiwat will be the Nation’s Doctor as the United States Surgeon General.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Trump picks Project 2025 author Russell Vought to lead budget office
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump tapped Project 2025 co-author Russell Vought to serve as the director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) during his second administration. Vought served as Trump's OMB deputy director during his first term in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: ICE Buffalo official takes shot at NY Gov Hochul after arrest of wanted illegal immigrant
item.description: Officials with Immigration and Customs Enforcement in Buffalo, New York, took a shot at New York Gov. Kathy Hochul's immigration policies while announcing the arrest of a wanted illegal migrant.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How NYC congestion pricing tolls could impact drivers
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York Gov. Kathy Hochul recently introduced a new plan to toll drivers who enter Manhattan below 60th Street. The revised congestion pricing plan would cost most drivers $9 during peak hours and $2.25 during off-peak hours. It's expected to go into effect in January.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: NJ Rep. Gottheimer vows to fight congestion pricing
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) — Rep. Josh Gottheimer has been a staunch opponent of congestion pricing since its introduction. Now, with a new plan set in motion by New York Gov.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Trump expected to name investor Scott Bessent as Treasury secretary pick
item.description: Bessent, founder and CEO of hedge fund Key Square Group, served as an economic adviser to the Trump campaign, often appearing on financial talk shows to argue in favor of the president-elect's economic policies.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California entrepreneur who led minimum wage measure 'disappointed' by defeat, vows to fight for working class
item.description: The backer of California's recently defeated minimum wage measure said he was "disappointed" but vowed to keep advocating for higher wages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge reduces prison sentence for Capitol rioter who berated and insulted him
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Friday imposed a one-year reduction in a prison sentence for a man who stormed the U.S. Capitol and then engaged in a pattern of disruptive courtroom behavior, including berating and insulting the judge.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republican who lost Minnesota House race sues claiming DFL winner lives outside district
item.description: The Roseville-area court challenge is the latest legal action Republicans are using to try and break the tie in the Minnesota House next year and muster up a majority in one chamber.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Gaetz says he will not return to Congress
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) said Friday he will not return to Congress after resigning his House seat last week, despite having withdrawn from consideration to be President-elect Trump’s attorney general — but added he is “not going anywhere.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans open to RFK Jr. at HHS despite abortion-rights stance
item.description: Senate Republicans are signaling that they are open to Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s nomination to lead the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) despite his long-standing support of abortion rights and concerns emanating from anti-abortion voices about his selection.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, NewsNation

item.title: Trump chooses Pam Bondi for attorney general pick after Gaetz withdraws
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Thursday named Pam Bondi, the former attorney general of Florida, to be U.S. attorney general just hours after his other choice, Matt Gaetz, withdrew his name.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
